text: Kahit ramdam pa ang epekto ng El Niño, naglabas na ang PAGASA ng La Niña watch. Posible kasing umiral ang La Niña o paglamig ng temperatura sa dagat, na nagreresulta sa malakas na ulan sa ikalawang bahagi ng taon. Nagpa-Patrol, Raphael Bosano. TV Patrol, Huwebes, 7 Marso 2024

Screenshot from Philippine Coast Guard video via Jay Tarriela/X
MANILA — A US official on Thursday called China's aggression in the South China Sea "unacceptable and destabilizing," adding its moves ran against international law.
US State Department Assistant Secretary for East Asian and Pacific Affairs Daniel Kritenbrink also accused Beijing of using "coercion to intimidate partners" as he criticized its fresh hostilities in the disputed waterway.
During an online press conference, Kritenbrink reiterated the US commitment to the Indo-Pacific region and emphasized the strategy of investing in the collective capacity of its partners.
"First, we continue to focus on our diplomacy, including with partners in ASEAN to make sure that all countries, including all claimants, base their claims on international law, that all claimants commit to recognizing and respecting freedom of navigation, freedom of overflight, and the peaceful resolution of disputes," he said.
The official then underscored the value of maritime domain awareness.
"The United States has invested hundreds of millions of dollars, in partners' maritime domain awareness capabilities because we believe that the more countries are able to understand what is happening in their maritime domains and defend their own interest in the maritime domains and further promote stability across the region," Kritenbrink said.
Kritenbrink also assured the Philippines of the US’ physical presence in the region.
"The United States continues through our own operations to be physically present, in the region on a daily basis, and we continue to fly, sell, and operate, everywhere that international law allows, demonstrating thereby that all countries share those same rights," he said.
"So our goal, again, is to promote peace and stability, security and prosperity, promote, respect for international law, peaceful resolution of disputes, but also ensure that countries have their own ability to protect their sovereignty and their own interests," he said.
Senator: Recall PH envoy to Beijing to show 'disgust' over sea attacks
The Philippines on Tuesday said China Coast Guard vessels caused 2 collisions with Manila boats and water cannoned one of them, leaving four Filipino crew members injured. 
Marcos: PH will not invoke defense treaty with US despite fresh sea tensions
The incident occurred in waters around Second Thomas Shoal in the Spratly Islands where the countries have contested maritime claims.
China's foreign ministry on Wednesday however said its coast guard was "restrained" in the recent clash.

Kritenbrink's statement comes after his agency condemned China's actions on Tuesday, which "show disregard for the safety and livelihoods of Filipinos and international law." 
US State Department spokesperson Matthew Miller said that an international tribunal in 2016 ruled that China has "no lawful maritime claims" to the waters around Ayungin, which is "clearly within the Philippines exclusive economic zone."
Beijing continues to ignore that ruling and insists on its vast claims in the South China Sea, where the West Philippine Sea is located.
China says US using Philippines as 'pawn' in South China Sea
Philippines foreign minister urges China: 'Stop harassing us'
—with reports from Agence France-Presse and Reuters

Seventeen examinees passed the Dental Technologist licensure board examination, according to the Professional  Regulation Commission.
Thirteen examinees came from De Ocampo Memorial College, three from National University Manila and one from University of the East.
Dirlene Medianesta Bellezas topped the exams.
The other successful examinees are:

The date and venue for the oathtaking ceremony of the new successful examinees in the said examination will be announced soon.

Naaalarma ang Commission on Human Rights sa sunod-sunod na pagpatay sa mga opisyal at kawani ng barangay sa bansa. Nanawagan rin ang komisyon para sa agarang resolusyon ng mga kaso. Nagpa-Patrol, Victoria Tulad. TV Patrol, Huwebes, 7 Marso 2024

The Palace has debunked a post making rounds online wherein it was claimed that March 11 was declared a "nationwide regular holiday."
According to the Official Gazette of the Republic of the Philippines, the post which showed an "official document" that was labeled as Proclamation No. 729" that purportedly declared Monday, March 11, 2024, as a "nationwide regular holiday in celebration of Eid’l Fitr."
"It is a tampered version of Proclamation No. 729, s. 2019, issued by then Executive Secretary Medialdea during the previous administration," the Official Gazette said.

A federal judge has granted the US government's request to unseal the arrest warrant and first superseding indictment in the US case of televangelist Apollo Quiboloy.
Terry J. Hatter Jr., US District judge of the US District Court for the Central District of California, granted on March 1 the request of the US government to unseal the arrest warrant and returns in the case of Quiboloy.
A warrant for arrest issued in November of 2021 commands the United States Marshal and any authorized United States officers to arrest Quiboloy on offenses of conspiracy to engage in sex trafficking by force, fraud and coercion and sex trafficking of children.
The offenses also include sex trafficking by force, fraud, or coercion as well as conspiracy and bulk cash smuggling.
The first superseding indictment was filed in April of 2021 and names Quiboloy, Teresita Dandan, Helen Panilag, Felina Salinas, Guia Cabactulan, Marissa Duenas, Amanda Estopare, Bettina Padilla Roses, and Maria De Leon as co-defendants.
The 74-page superseding indictment outlines the alleged scheme that Quiboloy and his co-defendants used to recruit minors, secure visas, engaged in marriage fraud and how victims were sexually abused and exploited for labor, and how money was transferred during these alleged crimes.
It also lists out 16 overt acts which includes sexual, physical, verbal, and emotional abuse of underage women committed by Quiboloy and others.
In the meantime ,the jury trial for Quiboloy, and several co-defendants have  has been continued to November of this year.
Quiboloy, Danan, and Panilag remain on the Federal Bureau of Investigation’s most wanted list.
US Treasury sanctions Quiboloy, around 40 others for 'corruption, human rights abuse'
FBI can't pursue Quiboloy without extradition: ex-UP Law dean

Limang senador ang unang pumirma sa sulat na gustong harangin ang contempt ruling ni Sen. Risa Hontiveros laban kay Kingdom of Jesus Christ Pastor Apollo Quiboloy. Pero isa sa kanila ay binawi ang pirma matapos isapubliko. Nagpa-Patrol, Sherrie Ann Torres. TV Patrol, Huwebes, 7 Marso 2024

People flock to a waterpark at the Pasig Rainforest Park in Pasig City on March 2, 2024 as temperatures rise in the metro. According to state weather bureau PAGASA, El Niño is expected to bring temperatures ranging from 36 to 40 degrees celsius to Metro Manila and northern Luzon. Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News
MANILA — While the heat outside may be searing, state weather bureau PAGASA said Thursday it still isn’t the dry season, which most people colloquially call summer.
PAGASA Weather Division chief Juanito Galang said the northeast monsoon is still prevailing, particularly in the northern portions of the country.
“Officially hindi pa natin dine-declare na terminated ang northeast monsoon,” he said.
Based on discussions with the climatology division, Galang said that onset of the dry season may be declared in two weeks.
Temperatures are forecast to surge in the coming months with maximums as high as 38 degrees Celsius in Northern Luzon this month.
The mercury is also expected to rise to as high as 40.7 degrees in Northern Luzon by May and June. The lowlands may see maximum temperatures one degree lower.
In Metro Manila, maximum heat is seen to reach 38 degrees or higher by April and May.
PAGASA said however, these figures do not include humidity, which leads to a much higher heat index.
PAGASA said the dry season temperatures and rainfall reduction are influenced by the ongoing El Niño.

Vice President Sara Duterte on Thursday said personal attacks meant to destroy her reputation have intensifed.
In a video statement, Duterte said these are part of a black propaganda and politicking from people who want to see her fail as a public servant.
“Nagiging mas matindi, mapangahas, at desperado na ang mga paninira sa akin ngayon,” she said.
“Bahagi ang mga ito ng isang organisadong demolition job na ang layunin ay sirain ang aking integridad at gumawa ng imahe na ako ay isang mamamatay-tao, na corrupt, abusado, taksil at isang war lord,” she added
She also enumerated in the video what she considered as personal attacks against her.
“Makikita natin ito sa pag-atake sa confidential funds, pagpapalaganap ng video sa Commonwealth traffic, paggawa ng issue sa pagtatag ng security para sa opisina ng Bise Presidente, paglabas ng testigo na umanoy ako ay kaparte ng Davao Death Squad, sa malisyosong ulat tungkol sa aking mga baril, at ang pambabastos sa relasyon namin ng aking asawa,” she said. 
“Kamakailan lamang ay nagsusulputan din ang mga online scam sa pera sa social media katulad ng scholarship o pabaon program para sa mga mag-aaral,” she added.
She said all these were done so that the public might lose their confidence in her as a public servant.
“Sinisiraan ako para pagdudahan, pahiyain, at panghinaan ng loob. Nais nilang isuko ko ang aking sinumpaang tungkulin na magsilbi sa bayan,” she added.
“Ayaw nilang magtagumpay tayo para sa edukasyon ng mga batang Pilipino at para sa kanilang kinabukasan na nanganganib sa kamay ng korapsyon, kriminalidad, iligal na droga, at terorismo,” Duterte said.
Duterte thanked her supporters for their trust and prayers.
 “Tandaan po natin na ang mga paninirang ito ay nagkukubli ng mga personal at politikal na interes. At ang mga ito ay hindi ninyo interes, hindi interes ng bayan, hindi ito interes ng Pilipinas,” she said.

Huli sa CCTV ang pamamaril sa isang mekaniko sa La Paz, Leyte. Sa video, isang lalaking nakamotorsiklo ang huminto malapit sa biktima at bigla na lang siyang pinaputukan. Agad na tumakas ang gunman habang kritikal ang kondisyon ng biktima.

An US State Department official expresses concern over China’s use of intimidation in its maritime row with the Philippines.
He made the comment as China accused the US of using the Philippines as a pawn to destabilize the South China Sea. - The World Tonight, ANC, March 7, 2024

A US federal judge paved the way for the arrest and possible extradition of Filipino televangelist Apollo Quiboloy.
But several Philippine lawmakers are trying to prevent his arrest over a contempt citation by the chair of a Senate panel investigating his alleged abuses. - The World Tonight, ANC, March 7, 2024

MANILA -- The Land Transportation Office (LTO) is now studying the possibility of rescinding its contract with German information technology contractor Dermalog, which is behind the agency’s online portal Land Transportation Management System (LTMS).
House Transportation Committee Chairman and Antipolo 2nd District Rep. Romeo Acop questioned the existing contract of LTO and Dermalog during its hearing on Thursday.
During the Transportation Committee meeting, LTO chief Assistant Secretary Vigor Mendoza II said they have consulted with the Office of the Solicitor general on the possible legal moves that be taken with regards to the Dermalog contract.
“We immediately convened with our lawyers with the Office of the Solicitor General in order for us to be guided as to the next legal steps and our legal options as far as the issues raised by the honorable congressman that includes the possibility of rescission and what would be the steps necessary should that be the direction given to us by the DOTr,” Mendoza said.
According to Mendoza, they also consulted with the Department of Information and Communications Technology (DICT) to make sure that public service, including operation of motor vehicle registration and licensing, will not be affected in case they push through with rescinding the contract.
“Our talks with DICT have been positive as far as making sure that public service is not affected. We are having a meeting next week ( with DOTr ) to determine our next steps,” Mendoza said.
“The DOTr agrees as far as the grounds for rescission of contract is concerned. They have reviewed, we have already provided them with COA report as early as last year,"he added.
The Solicitor General’s preliminary opinion, according to Mendoza, is that there are grounds for contract rescission.
“Before actual decision the contract provides for a mandatory arbitration proceedings,” Mendoza explained.
During the previous committee meeting, SAGIP Party-list Rep. Rodante Marcoleta cited the latest Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) of the Commission on Audit (COA), revealed that the actual delivery period of milestones under the LTMS project from Dermalog exceeded the allowable time extension, which was not compliant with the Government Procurement Manual Volume 2.
The COA reported that Milestones 1 and 3 of the LTMS were delayed from 165 to 756 days, respectively, despite multiple deadline extensions.
According to Mendoza, LTO does not have full access to the LTMS, so they could not make any modifications to the system without the assistance of Dermalog.
“I was just informed by our MID that we do not have full access to database. Our MID is here, they do it day to day…they can only view the data, the cannot modify the data as we want it,” Mendoza said.
The notice of award to Dermalog was given on May 17, 2018 and the notice to proceed was given on May 28, 2018 for 6.5 months to complete the project and 5 years maintenance contract according to Mendoza.
But Dermalog’s legal counsel and resident agent to the Philippines, Atty. Anthony Peralta, explained that the turnover of the database would be done next year based on their contract with LTO.
“Together with the source code, the turnover of the database shall be done at the end of the contract which is at the end of the maintenance period which was confirmed by the representative from COA would be next year in 2025. As of the present, LTO has access to the LTMS database. It’s just that they don’t have the means to modify,” Peralta explained.
Acop agreed with LTO chief that the turnover of source code for developed application modules would be owned by the procuring entity and shall be turned over to them immediately upon the completion of the project and not of the contract. 
“That is my position but the country representative of Dermalog has a different interpretation. So siguro mas maganda talaga i-pursue ninyo yung termination or rescission nung kontrata, otherwise I will make this an issue,” Acop said.

Tiniyak ng Armed Forces of the Philippines na tuloy ang rotation and resupply missions sa Ayungin Shoal. Ito'y kahit pa napinsala ang mga ginagamit na supply boat dahil sa pagbangga at pagbomba ng water cannon ng mga barko ng China. Pinayuhan naman ng Beijing ang mga bansa sa labas ng rehiyon na huwag magsimula ng gulo sa South China Sea. Nagpa-Patrol, Bianca Dava. TV Patrol, Huwebes, 7 Marso 2024

Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. backed the idea of Australia joining the Association of Southeast Asian Nations.
But he’s concerned about how maritime tensions with China might affect the Philippines’ planned drills with Australia. - The World Tonight, ANC, March 7, 2024

Arestado ang isang lalaki sa Bacoor, Cavite na nang-blackmail umano ng isang dalaga. Tinakot umano ang biktima na ikakalat ang maseselan niyang video kung hindi makikipagkita sa suspek. Sinabi naman ng suspek na may iba pa siyang mga nabiktima sa parehong modus. Nagpa-Patrol, Niko Baua. TV Patrol, Huwebes, 7 Marso 2024

Hindi lang init ng panahon ang dapat isaalang-alang sa umiiral na El Niño kundi maging ang posibleng sakit mula sa mahinang pressure at maling pag-iimbak ng tubig. May paalala rin ang mga eksperto at opisyal, lalo't nakapagtala ang Department of Health ng daan-daang kaso ng water contamination. Nagpa-Patrol, Arra Perez. TV Patrol, Huwebes, 7 Marso 2024
